mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate held near the zero lower bound (0.12%) reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and lowers the opportunity cost of holding risk assets relative to cash, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns as long as the rate remains suppressed.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.12 percent, near historic lows
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns during low-rate periods are not statistically higher than the full-sample mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over a rolling 3-month window signals an inflecting labor market and rising recession probability, which historically precedes equity drawdowns as forward earnings expectations are revised down over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp over trailing 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.6 percent, elevated relative to cycle lows
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the effect is not significant at p > 0.10 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly indicate a broad-based consumer and residential investment slowdown that single-series signals miss, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as aggregate demand weakens.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM decline AND HOUST MoM decline concurrently
failure_condition: Joint decline episodes show no significant difference in mean forward 20d return versus episodes where only one series declines (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC of the joint signal is below that of either single-series signal.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding real economic output and corporate revenue capacity, which is rapidly priced into equities within a one-week horizon as a leading confirmation of business-cycle strength.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO MoM growth and forward 5d returns is below 0.02 over any rolling 1-year window, or sign concordance rate falls below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-lower-bound monetary regime (FEDFUNDS below 0.5%), rising CPI inflation is interpreted by markets as a precursor to policy normalization rather than as a demand-driven risk, causing forward S&P 500 returns to react negatively to CPI upside surprises only in this regime, unlike in a normal-rate regime where such moves are muted.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS below 0.5% and CPIAUCSL YoY acceleration
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary regime with rising inflation
failure_condition: The negative return response to CPI acceleration under ZLB conditions is not significantly stronger than under normal-rate regimes (interaction coefficient p > 0.10), or Rank IC does not exceed the single-series FEDFUNDS-only baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payroll growth decelerates while the unemployment rate simultaneously rises, the combined signal indicates a genuine labor market turning point rather than noisy monthly volatility, producing a stronger and more persistent negative equity response over a quarterly horizon than either indicator alone.
failure_condition: Joint PAYEMS deceleration and UNRATE rise episodes do not produce forward 60d returns significantly more negative than episodes with only one condition met (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts below 1000 thousand units signals contraction in residential construction, a historically leading component of aggregate demand, which typically precedes a broader economic slowdown and negative equity performance over the subsequent month.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1028 thousand units, near threshold
failure_condition: SPX forward 20d returns following HOUST breaches below 1000 are not significantly lower than the unconditional mean (p > 0.10), or the effect reverses in more than 50% of episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) reflects durable expansion in aggregate output and corporate earnings capacity, supporting a persistent positive drift in equity prices over a quarterly forward horizon as long as growth remains above trend.
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 QoQ growth and forward 60d returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or positive GDP growth periods do not show significantly higher mean forward returns (p > 0.05).
